channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Judge Blocks Release of Video Evidence in Abbotsford Double Murder Trial
item.description: A British Columbia judge has denied a request to release video evidence from the 2022 Abbotsford double murder trial, despite the victim's family urging transparency. The video, which shows suspects a...

item.title: NATO Commits €70 Billion in Aid to Ukraine Amid Russia's Strong Criticism
item.description: The article discusses the NATO summit in Turkey where member nations pledged €70 billion in military aid to Ukraine for 2026, reaffirming their commitment to collective defense under Article 5. Russia...

item.title: News Corp Australia Blocks Bot Access with Instructions for Users
item.description: The article details a message from News Corp Australia regarding bot detection on their websites. The company uses software to manage crawler traffic, and users experiencing access issues are advised ...

item.title: Government Allocates Rs2bn for HIV-Affected Children's Treatment and Disciplinary Actions
item.description: The Sindh Employees’ Social Security Institution (SESSI) has allocated Rs2 billion to establish an endowment fund for the long-term medical treatment and welfare of 78 children infected with HIV/AIDS ...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs and Orlando Pirates Begin Pre-Season Tours in Spain
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs and Orlando Pirates are departing for Spain to embark on their pre-season tours, marking the first time both teams will train abroad. The Soweto Giants will be based at the Pinatar Arena...

item.title: Ashley St. Clair says Turning Point USA is struggling to move forward after Charlie Kirk's death
item.description: Former MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair sharply criticized the future of Turning Point USA during an appearance on The Daily Beast Podcast, arguing that the conservative youth organization has signifi...

item.title: Two suspects arrested after failed cash-in-transit robbery and confrontation in Gauteng
item.description: Gauteng police have arrested two men, aged 47 and 52, in connection with a failed armed robbery attempt and a violent confrontation with a security officer in Silverton. The incident occurred on July ...

item.title: National Security Dialogue in Nigeria Aims to Address Insecurity Challenges
item.description: A high-level national security dialogue organized by First Daily has been announced, bringing together military leaders, politicians, and cultural figures from across Nigeria. The event aims to addres...

item.title: Saskatoon Farm Faces Loss After Hailstorm Destroys Strawberry Crops
item.description: A Saskatoon-area farm has suffered significant losses after a severe hailstorm devastated its strawberry crops, marking a challenging end to a difficult growing season. Manager Chloe Kook described th...

item.title: Rare 450-Million-Year-Old Crinoid Fossil Reveals Soft Tissue Insights
item.description: Researchers discovered a rare crinoid fossil from approximately 450 million years ago, marking the second known instance of soft tissue preservation in such ancient sea creatures. The fossil, belongin...

item.title: Pakistan and US to Discuss Tariff Relief and Bilateral Trade Agreement
item.description: Pakistan and the United States are set to resume two-day trade negotiations in Washington, focusing on tariff reductions and a broader bilateral trade agreement. The talks follow Trump-era tariffs imp...

item.title: Man Admits Faking Kidnap to Demand ₦25m from Family
item.description: A Delta State man, Michael James, confessed to staging his own kidnapping to demand ₦25 million from his family. He claimed he owed money to his siblings and couldn't repay them, so he faked his abduc...

item.title: Government to Enhance Airport Facilities for Passengers
item.description: Home Minister Amit Shah has directed all 62 major airports in India to align with Bureau of Civil Aviation Security (BCAS) standards within two years, aiming to boost passenger convenience while maint...

item.title: Badrinath Temple Donation Probe: FIR Against Suspended Employee Over Financial Irregularities
item.description: Police in Chamoli district have registered an FIR against Pramod Nautiyal, a suspended employee of the Shri Badrinath Kedarnath Temple Committee (BKTC), over alleged financial irregularities in handli...

item.title: ABS-CBN Lifestyle Announces Word of the Day for July 9, 2026: diha
item.description: The article highlights ABS-CBN Lifestyle's Word of the Day feature for July 9, 2026, which focuses on the Filipino word 'diha' meaning 'there' or 'in'. It emphasizes the publication's role as a go-to ...

item.title: Public broadcasters address antisemitism royal commission and Pauline Hanson's involvement
item.description: The article discusses the recent statements and submissions by the SBS and ABC regarding the antisemitism royal commission. Both broadcasters are set to appear today, with SBS releasing a submission t...

item.title: Tennis: Kostyuk advances to Wimbledon semifinals after victory over Noskova
item.description: The article discusses the progression of Ukrainian tennis player Marta Kostyuk in the Wimbledon tournament, detailing her recent match against Czech opponent Markéta Vondroušová (Noskova). Following a...

item.title: UK PM receives decommissioned revolver from Turkey after NATO summit
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer received a personalised revolver with live ammunition as a gift from Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan during the NATO summit in Ankara. The firearm, engraved with...

item.title: Lovable's Valuation Doubles to $13.2 Billion as Vibe-Coding Gains Traction
item.description: Lovable, a Swedish startup specializing in vibe-coding technology, is reportedly in advanced negotiations to raise $300 million at a valuation of $13.2 billion—exactly double its $6.6 billion valuatio...

item.title: India and Afghanistan to form Joint Working Group for Agriculture and Livestock Collaboration
item.description: India and Afghanistan have agreed to establish a Joint Working Group to develop a long-term roadmap for cooperation in agriculture and livestock. The decision was made during bilateral talks between U...
